titlestmt.author: Compiled by Carol King, Nancy Lyon, and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:18

did.unittitle: Mauritius collection

descriptivenote.p: The materials are in French, English, and French Creole.

did.abstract: The collection consists of manuscripts and printed material relating to the history of Mauritius and current political, economic, and social conditions in Mauritius.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the African Collection Curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Mauritius Collection (MS 1531). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: African Collection, MS 605; Pamphlet Collection, MS 1351; African Postcard Collection, MS 1575. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in the original accession is arranged by category and chronologically therein. Additions to the collection may vary in arrangement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of manuscripts and printed material relating to the history of Mauritius and current political, economic, and social conditions in Mauritius.

did.unittitle: Original accession
Journals: cultural and educational
Periodicals: magazines; newspapers; popular journals
General information pamphlets; brochures; booklets
Women's movement/organizations: journals; newsletters; printed material
Photograph
Additional Material

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged by type and chronologically therein.

did.unittitle: General information brochures
Government documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The government documents presented at the various courts include appeals, ratifications and/or amendments of ordinances and orders, and the makeup of the Constitution of the government council.

did.unittitle: At the Court of Saint James's
At the Court at Windsor

unitdatestructured.altrender: 1842 January 15, 1877 July 11, 1879 June 26, 1880 March 18, 1890 November 22, 1894 December 12
unitdatestructured.label: creation

did.unittitle: At the Court at Osborne House, Isle of Wight
At the Court at Buckingham Palace
Government memoranda and reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Government memoranda and reports relate to one specific topic, unlike the documents presented at the various courts which cover a multitude of topics.

did.unittitle: "Report of the Currency Notes Commission, 1935-1936"
"Memorandum on Mauritius Development and Welfare: Ten Year Plan"
"Memorandum on Social Welfare"
"Social Insurance Schemes in Mauritius"
"Report on Secondary Education in Mauritius, October 1947"
"The Production of Vegetable Seeds in Mauritius (1943-1946)"
"Report on the Housing of Sugar Estate Workers"
"Harbour Guide, 1940"
"Memorandum on Long Term Policy, Food Production Board"

"Report of a Committee appointed for the purpose of investigating the Question of relieving the Police Department of Revenue and Liquor Control and of the collection of Direct Taxes and Licences, Sessional Paper No. 1 of 1951, Legislative Council"
"Report of the Committee on the Future of the Sack Factory, Sessional Paper No. 5 of 1951, Legislative Council"
"Report of the Committee appointed to consider the possibility of establishing a Planters" Mechanical Equipment Fund, Sessional Paper No. 6 of 1951, Legislative Council"
"Salaries Commission, Second Interim Report, Cost of Living Allowances, Sessional Paper No. 8 of 1951"
"Cost of Living Allowance, Governor's despatch of 30th May and views of the Secretary of State contained in his telegram of 8th June, Sessional Paper No. 9 of 1951, Legislative Council"
"Report of the Committee appointed to review the conditions governing the payment of Outdoor Relief, Sessional Paper No. 2 of 1952, Legislative Council"
"Liquor Policy of the Mauritius Government, Sessional Paper No. 7 of 1952, Legislative Council"
"Education Officers' Conference 1951: The Road Ahead"
"The Historical Monuments of Mauritius"
"Report on Setting Up a Port Authority, October 1972"
"Mauritius-Resettlement of Persons Transferred From Chagos Archipelago: Visit to Mauritius From 24 January to 2 February"
"Industrial Dispute Between Tyre Retreading Factories Workers' Union and Retreaders Ltd."
Two Visits to Rodrigues, 1948 and 1949, with H.E. Sir Hilary Blood , by Alfred North-Coombes
Voice of Mauritians (official magazine of the Association for the Promotion of the Welfare of Mauritians in the U.K.)
Additional material
Printed material
